company_description: The ByteDance Seed team is dedicated to pioneering paths toward artificial general intelligence.
job_description: Responsibilities: Assist in building and optimizing large-scale distributed training systems. Qualifications: Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science. Proficiency in Python or C++. Familiarity with PyTorch.

bullets.text: Engineered a high-performance data preprocessing pipeline in Python to handle 50GB of streaming data, utilizing k-core filtering and Parquet storage to streamline infrastructure for large-scale model training.
Implemented eight distinct deep learning architectures using PyTorch, optimizing GPU memory management and training loops to achieve a state-of-the-art HR@10 of 0.8808 on the HotelRec dataset.
Optimized model ranking performance by developing a stacked meta-ensemble with LambdaRank, leveraging automated grid search and YAML-driven configurations to manage complex experimentation.
Built a reproducible research infrastructure for a three-person team, featuring fully scripted pipelines and seed-fixed randomness to ensure consistency across distributed development tracks.
Engineered a high-throughput ML inference pipeline using Python and PyTorch, optimizing cascaded model execution via ONNX to achieve real-time processing of concurrent video streams.
Architected scalable distributed infrastructure on AWS using ECS Fargate and Terraform, leveraging Redis for state management to ensure low-latency synchronization across distributed nodes.

Optimized backend service performance by implementing Docker multi-stage builds and Gunicorn-managed Flask APIs, reducing deployment overhead and enhancing system reliability for large-scale workloads.
Built an automated MLOps workflow for model versioning and S3-based distribution, streamlining the deployment of optimized computer vision models across a distributed computing environment.
Engineered a C-level LD_PRELOAD shim and socket handling layer to optimize low-level system interactions, improving communication efficiency for distributed AI agent environments.
Built a modular LLM evaluation harness using Python and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to automate performance benchmarking across 11 specialized domains, ensuring production-grade reliability.

Implemented high-performance data processing modules for complex formats including OOXML and PDF using Python, incorporating defensive programming and safe XML parsing to support large-scale document ingestion for model training.

Architected a scalable AI development ecosystem with 11 domain-specific agents, integrating Anthropic APIs and reusable Python skills to assist in the rapid prototyping and optimization of foundation model infrastructure.

Engineered a scalable data processing pipeline in Python to ingest and normalize 13.59M Vector Covariance Message (VCM) snapshots, supporting large-scale training for 19,300 cataloged orbital objects.
Architected a hybrid physics-ML framework using PyTorch to replace deterministic models with 3D voxel-based occupancy maps, enhancing the precision of probabilistic risk assessment.

Optimized high-fidelity orbital simulations by implementing RK4 propagation and J2 perturbation algorithms in C++, significantly improving the throughput of the training data generation infrastructure.

Designed a robust feature engineering suite comprising 41 physics-informed parameters across 7 groups, pivoting research strategy to resolve model convergence issues in complex trajectory predictions.
Engineered a learned video compression pipeline using asymmetric autoencoders in PyTorch, achieving a 57–73× compression ratio on the VIRAT aerial surveillance dataset.
Architected a high-performance streaming infrastructure using Kafka and binary serialization to process video residuals with sub-100ms latency for real-time inference.
Optimized rate-distortion trade-offs via a custom Group-of-Pictures (GOP) framework, reaching 28.37 dB PSNR and 0.820 SSIM at 0.656 BPP for high-fidelity reconstruction.
Built an end-to-end data ingestion and preprocessing pipeline using Python and FFmpeg to manage 4.9 GB of video data, streamlining large-scale training for AI research.
